content.problemStatement:
You have a knapsack that can take upto WmaxW_{max}Wmax​ weight.

Given NNN items, ithi^{th}ith of them having the weight WiW_iWi​ and value ViV_iVi​. 

You can pick some or all the items completely so that their weight does not exceed WmaxW_{max}Wmax​.

Your task is to maximise the total value of the items you will carry in your bag.


Input Format

The first line contains two integer NNN and WmaxW_{max}Wmax​, denoting the total number of items and capacity of the knapsack
Next NNN lines contains the description of the items.

ithi^{th}ith line contains two integers WiW_iWi​ and ViV_iVi​, denoting the Weight and Value of the ithi^{th}ith item




Output Format
Output the maximum value you can carry while not exceeding the weight limit of the knapsack.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and Wmax — the number of items and the maximum weight the knapsack can hold. Then N lines follow, each containing two integers Wi and Vi — the weight and value of the i‑th item.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum total value achievable without exceeding the weight limit.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Choosing items (6,5), (5,6) and (3,5) gives weight 14 and value 16. Replacing (6,4) with (6,6) yields weight 14 and value 17, which is optimal.
Only the first item fits within capacity 3, giving value 4.
Best combination is items (4,7) and (6,12) with total weight 10 and total value 19.

solution.approach: The problem is the classic 0/1 knapsack where each item can be taken at most once. Since N ≤ 15 but Wmax can be up to 10^4, a DP over weight is efficient. We maintain dp[c] = maximum value achievable with total weight at most c. Initialise dp[0]=0 and others to 0. For each item (wi, vi) we iterate capacities from Wmax down to wi and update dp[cap] = max(dp[cap], dp[cap‑wi] + vi). The answer for a test case is dp[Wmax]. This runs in O(N·Wmax) time and O(Wmax) memory per test case, easily fitting the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * Wmax) per test case, O(T * N * Wmax) overall.
